questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Number of metal ions characterized by flame test among the following is ________.

$$\mathrm{Sr}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ba}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}, \mathrm{Cu}^{2+}, \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Co}^{2+}, \mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$$
questions.solution:
All the following metal ions will respond to flame test.

$$\mathrm{Sr}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ba}^{2+}, \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}, \mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: During the detection of acidic radical present in a salt, a student gets a pale yellow precipitate soluble with difficulty in $$\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$$ solution when sodium carbonate extract was first acidified with dil. $$\mathrm{HNO}_3$$ and then $$\mathrm{AgNO}_3$$ solution was added. This indicates presence of :
questions.solution:
The $$\mathrm{Br}^{-}$$ ion present in the salt gives pale yellow ppt. of $$\mathrm{AgBr}$$ with $$\mathrm{AgNO}_3$$, insoluble in dil $$\mathrm{HNO}_3$$ but partially soluble in aq. $$\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$$.

$$\mathrm{Br}^{-}+\mathrm{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow \underset{\text { Pale yellow }}{\mathrm{AgBr} \downarrow}+\mathrm{NO}_3^{-}$$

$$\mathrm{AgBr}+2 \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH} \rightarrow \underset{\text { Partially soluble }}{\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_2\right] \mathrm{Br}}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: When a salt is treated with sodium hydroxide solution it gives gas X . On passing gas X through reagent Y a brown coloured precipitate is formed. X and Y respectively, are
questions.solution: $\mathrm{NH}_3$ is identify by $\mathrm{K}_2\left[\mathrm{HgI}_4\right]+\mathrm{KOH}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: During halogen test, sodium fusion extract is boiled with concentrated HNO3 to
options: decompose cyanide or sulphide of sodium
questions.solution:
During test for halogen, if nitrogen or sulphur is also
present in the compound, then sodium fusion
extract is first boiled with concentrated nitric acid to
decompose cyanide or sulphide of sodium formed
during Lassaigne's test.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: A solution when diluted with $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ and boiled, it gives a white precipitate. On addition of excess $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl} / \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$, the volume of precipitate decreases leaving behind a white gelatinous precipitate. Identify the precipitate which dissolves in $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH} / \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$.
questions.solution:
When water is added to the solution of $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}$ ions followed by boiling, the reaction between $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}$ ions and water occur with the formation of white precipitate of $\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH})_2$.

$$ \mathrm{Zn}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \rightarrow \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH})_2+2 \mathrm{H}^{+} $$

When excess of $\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl} / \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ is added to this precipitate, the precipitate gets dissolved due to formation of tetrammine zinc (II) complex.

$$ \begin{aligned} \mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{OH})_2+ & \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}+\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl} \rightarrow\left[\mathrm{Zn}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\right]^{2+}+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{OH}^{-} \end{aligned} $$

The complex is soluble.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : Griss-Ilosvay test is used for the detection of nitrite ion, which involves the use of sulphanilic acid and $\alpha$-naphthylamine reagent.

Statement II : In the above test, sulphanilic acid is diazotized by the acidified nitrite ion, which on further coupling with $\alpha$-naphthylamine forms an azo-dye.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options:
Statement I is true but Statement II is false

Both Statement I and Statement II are false

Statement I is false but Statement II is true

Both Statement I and Statement II are true
questions.solution:
In Gries–Ilosvay test for nitrite ion $\left(\text{NO}_2^-\right)$, the reagents used are sulphanilic acid and $\alpha$-naphthylamine.

  • In acidic medium, nitrite ion forms nitrous acid $HNO_2$, which diazotizes sulphanilic acid to give a diazonium salt.

  • This diazonium salt then couples with $\alpha$-naphthylamine to form a coloured azo dye (usually reddish/pink).

So, Statement I is true and Statement II is also true.

Correct option: D (Both Statement I and Statement II are true)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Formation of $\mathrm{Na}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5 \mathrm{NOS}\right]$, a purple coloured complex formed by addition of sodium nitroprusside in sodium carbonate extract of salt indicates the presence of :
options:
Sulphide ion
 Sulphite ion
 Sulphate ion
 Sodium ion
questions.solution: $\underset{\substack{\text { Sodium } \\ \text { sulphide }}}{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{S}}+\underset{\substack{\text { Sodium nitro } \\ \text { Prusside }}}{\mathrm{Na}_2\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5 \mathrm{NO}\right]}\rightarrow \underset{\substack{\text { Violet Colour } \\ }}{\mathrm{Na}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5 \mathrm{NOS}\right]}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
A solution of m-chloronailimne, m-chlorophenol and m-chlorobenzoic acid in ethyal acetate was extracted initially with a saturated solution of NaHCO<sub>3</sub>
 to give fraction A. The left over organic phase
was extracted with dilute NaoH solution tio give fraction B. The final organiic layer was labelled as
fraction C. Fractions A,B and C, contain respectively :
questions.solution:
m-chlorobenzoic acid dissolves in aq NaHCO<sub>3</sub>
while m-chlorophenol dissolves in aq NaOH.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match the following :

Test/Method Reagent
(i) Lucas Test (a) C6H5SO2Cl/ aq. KOH
(ii) Dumas method (b) HNO3/ AgNO3
(iii) Kjeldahl’s method (c) CuO/CO2
(iv) Hinsberg test (d) Conc. HCl and ZnCl2
(e) H2SO4
questions.solution:
Lucas reagent – Conc. HCl/ZnCl2

Dumas method – CuO/CO2

Kjeldahl’s method – H2SO4

Hinsberg test – C6H5SO2Cl/aq. KOH

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution:
The compound X is H$$_2$$S:

$$\mathrm{\mathop {N{a_2}S}\limits_{(X)} + 2{H^ + } \to {H_2}S + 2N{a^ + }}$$

Thus, the compound Y is FeCl$$_3$$.

Compound Y on reaction with potassium hyxacyanoferrate(II) forms intense blue precipitate which dissolves on addition of reagent

$$\mathrm{4FeC{l_3} + 3{K_4}\left[ {Fe(II){{(CN)}_6}} \right] \to \mathop {F{e_4}{{[Fe{{(CN)}_6}]}_3}}\limits_{Intense\,blue\,ppt.} + 12KCl}$$

Compound Y on reaction with potassium hexacyanoferrate (III) forms brown colouration due to

$$\mathrm{Fe[Fe(CN)_6]}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: During sodium nitroprusside test of sulphide ion in an aqueous solution, one of the ligands coordinated to the metal ion is converted to
questions.solution:
Sodium nitroprusside test is used for detection of sulphur in organic compound.

$\stackrel{+}{\mathrm{NO}}$ ligand is converted into $\mathrm{NOS}^{-}$ligand.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.solution: Pb salts form white precipitate of PbCl2 with dil. HCl, which dissolves in hot water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II

LIST I
(Name of the test)		 LIST II
(Reaction sequence involved) [M is metal]
A. Borax bead test I. $$\mathrm{MCO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{MO} \xrightarrow[+\Delta]{\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2} \mathrm{CoO} \cdot \mathrm{MO}$$
B. Charcoal cavity test II. $$\mathrm{MCO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{MCl}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{M}^{2+}$$
C. Cobalt nitrate test III. $$\mathrm{MSO}_4 \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~B}_4 \mathrm{O}_7} \mathrm{M}\left(\mathrm{BO}_2\right)_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{MBO}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{M}$$
D. Flame test IV. $$\mathrm{MSO}_4 \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3} \mathrm{MCO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{MO} \rightarrow \mathrm{M}$$

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options:
A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II
A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II
A-III, B-II, C-IV, D-I
A-III, B-I, C-II, D-IV
questions.solution:
(A) Borax bead test

$$\mathrm{MSO}_4 \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~B}_4 \mathrm{O}_7} \mathrm{M}\left(\mathrm{BO}_2\right)_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{MBO}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{M}$$

(B) Charcoal cavity test

$$\mathrm{MSO}_4 \xrightarrow[\Delta]{\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3} \mathrm{MCO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{MO} \rightarrow \mathrm{M}$$

(C) Cobalt nitrate test

$$\mathrm{MCO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{MO} \xrightarrow[+\Delta]{\mathrm{CO}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2} \mathrm{CoO} \cdot \mathrm{MO}$$

(D) Flame test

$$\mathrm{MCO}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{MCl}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{M}^{2+}$$

So, $$\mathrm{A} \rightarrow$$ (III), $$\mathrm{B} \rightarrow$$ (IV), $$\mathrm{C} \rightarrow$$ (I), $$\mathrm{D} \rightarrow$$ (II).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
To an aqueous solution containing ions such as Al3+, Zn2+, Ca2+, Fe3+, Ni2+, Ba2+ and Cu2+ was added conc. HCl, followed by H2S.

The total number of cations precipitated during this reaction is/are :
questions.solution:
Al3+ and Fe3+ sulphides hydrolyse in water.

Ni2+ and Zn2+ require basic medium with H2S to form ppt

Ca2+ and Ba2+ sulphides are soluble

hence, we will receive only CuS ppt.
